mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in new home sales and housing starts indicates a collapsing housing demand cycle, which transmits to broader equity valuations through financial sector stress and reduced consumer wealth, producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with a Sharpe ratio above 0.1, or positive returns dominate during housing downturns.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, elevated CPI readings trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 55% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 205, or the signal shows no statistical significance in regime-filtered backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening corporate output and capacity utilization, which directly supports earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with forward returns with a Rank IC below 0.01, or negative returns dominate during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment gain exceeding 150,000 signals robust labor market recovery and rising household income, which fuels consumer spending and generates positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 65% of months where payroll gains exceed 150,000, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate declines while the unemployment rate remains stable or falls, it indicates a soft landing policy stance that supports equity valuations without triggering recession fears, producing positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The combination of falling rates and stable unemployment yields negative returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarterly GDP growth confirms a contraction in aggregate demand, which forces corporate earnings revisions downward and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of quarters following negative GDP growth readings, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.
